pattern: Concatenate the two words and run longest-palindromic-subsequence DP, but only count answers whose matched outer pair straddles the boundary so both words contribute.
hints: Glue the two words into one string and think about palindromic subsequences of the whole thing.
A valid construction needs at least one character drawn from each original word.
Run the interval DP for longest palindromic subsequence on the combined string, and whenever the two equal endpoints lie on opposite sides of the join, that length is a candidate answer.

edge_inputs.description: no shared character, answer is zero
palindrome fully inside one word does not count

stress.description: two random strings over a small alphabet to encourage matches
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:rng.randrange(2, 5)]
    n1 = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    n2 = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size) + 1)
    w1 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n1))
    w2 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n2))
    return [w1, w2]
